name: China
summary: Day 78 of Hormuz blockade. **TRUMP IMMEDIATELY REVERSED POST-SUMMIT** ('decimation to be continued' Truth Social + Hannity 'not patient' + Detroit News 'didn't ask China for any favours' — directly contradicting Wednesday Fox claim Xi offered to broker peace); on **May 16 Trump confirmed new nuclear ultimatum letter to Iran**. Thursday joint statement produced **ZERO operational follow-through** — no HEU-custodian framework, no Chinese security guarantees, no verification architecture. **China silence post-summit (no MFA pushback on Trump's reversal)** is the structural reading: Xi has NOT publicly accepted any custodian role, China MFA preserved 'legitimate right to peaceful use of nuclear energy' escape valve, and China is choosing not to engage Trump's contradictory Fox/Truth Social rhetoric. The China-as-HEU-custodian door yesterday's brief recorded as 'soft-confirmed at rhetorical-alignment level' is now **operationally empty** — the joint statement WAS the deliverable, not a step toward one. **OPERATIONAL DEVELOPMENT: Iran allowed 30+ Chinese ships to transit Hormuz since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols'** (REPORTED — Fars/IRGC-Navy senior official, Iran state TV, Xinhua 'Iran begins letting some Chinese ships cross Strait of Hormuz' — Chinese state amplification, Al Jazeera, CBS, Defense Post, Newsweek). Defense Post and Newsweek noted Iran has occasionally allowed Chinese ships even during the broader blockade because China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil. Xinhua framing the story prominently signals Beijing treating Iranian permission as a state-success narrative — consistent with Xi's 'interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait' joint-statement line, while operationally relying on selective Iranian transit. **Chinese state press (TASS/RT/Xinhua/Global Times) all carried joint statement on respective state's terms and ran Chinese-vessels-transit story prominently**; bipartisan-support framing on Trump-Xi has weakened on US side for first time in cycle (Detroit News leading 'didn't ask for favours' contradiction). USD/CNY 6.81 (+0.3% 1D); USD/CNY fix May 15 at 6.8415 vs Reuters estimate 6.7976 = weaker-than-expected fix signaling Beijing tolerance for further yuan depreciation. Shanghai Composite 4,135.39 (-1.0% 1D); Hang Seng 25,962.73 (-1.6% 1D) — disappointment read absorbed but limited damage as China-stimulus skepticism cited as part of broader Friday risk-off (copper -4.8%, MSCI EM -3.4%). **B1 OFAC Economic Fury extended** (five Chinese teapot refineries prepared sanctions; China MOFCOM May 2 Blocking Rules invocation) carries forward — sanctions architecture remains 'explicit legal collision' even within joint-statement de-escalation tone. PRIOR (May 15): **TRUMP-XI SUMMIT DAY 2 PRODUCED JOINT STATEMENT THAT IRAN 'CAN NEVER HAVE A NUCLEAR WEAPON' AND STRAIT OF HORMUZ 'MUST REMAIN OPEN'** (CONFIRMED multi-cluster — Times of Israel May 15 liveblog primary, CBS News, CNN Politics, China MFA official readout fmprc.gov.cn 2026-05-14, Trump Fox News interview). Both sides committed to a 'constructive China-U.S. relationship of strategic stability' framework guiding the next three years. **Xi additionally opposed 'the militarization of the Strait' and any effort to 'charge a toll' for its use, and said China is interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait** — operationally meaningful given >50% of Chinese energy transits Hormuz today. **The China MFA readout records that 'China values Iran's pledge not to pursue nuclear weapons while affirming its legitimate right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy'** — preserves Iran's right-to-civilian-program escape valve in the language. The HEU-via-China custodian door yesterday's brief opened is now soft-confirmed at rhetorical-alignment level; neither side announced an operational custodian framework, Xi has NOT publicly accepted any custodian role. Trump's Fox News follow-on assertions (CLAIMED — single source, NOT in joint statement text or MFA readout): Xi 'offered to broker peace with Iran,' 'won't supply Iran military equipment,' committed to buying soybeans/oil/LNG + 200 Boeing 737s — analyst skepticism widespread because China's existing soybean appetite is limited by cheap Brazilian alternatives (Reuters via People News Today). Xinhua and Global Times carried the joint statement on China's terms — Beijing letting the meeting + strategic-stability framework speak rather than commenting on Trump's Fox News claims. **PRIOR (May 14): Day 1 of summit — Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door; Xi opened with 'Thucydides Trap' framing + wants 2026 'landmark year.'** B1 OFAC Economic Fury extended (five Chinese teapot refineries prepared sanctions; China MOFCOM May 2 Blocking Rules invocation) carries forward; sanctions architecture remains 'explicit legal collision' even within joint-statement de-escalation tone. USD/CNY fix May 15 at 6.8415 vs Reuters estimate 6.7976 = weaker-than-expected fix, signaling Beijing tolerance for further yuan depreciation; Shanghai Composite 4,121.01 (-1.4% 1D); Hang Seng 25,897.05 (-1.9% 1D) — Hang Seng Trump-Xi 'disappointment' read (Foreign Policy: 'failed to secure major concessions'). Tasnim's Velayati 'we defeated you on the battlefield' jab carries forward — Iran framing Beijing summit as moment of Iranian leverage, not weakness. MATERIAL POLICY SIGNAL — contradicts pre-departure 'doesn't need Xi' line, substitutes China for Russia as proposed HEU custodian on Day 15 of 21 Russia-track window (US officials had reportedly ruled out Russia-as-custodian). Xi opened with 'Thucydides Trap' framing + 'partners rather than rivals' + wants 2026 'landmark year' — language designed to lower temperature. Xi has NOT publicly accepted any custodian role; framework remains stalled on substance (Iran's HEU return-clause demand + Trump's text-light approach unchanged) — but public custodian door now re-opened in different jurisdiction. **B5 IRAN'S AMBASSADOR TO CHINA PUBLICLY FRAMED BEIJING AS 'POLITICAL BALANCING' PARTNER** (Tasnim primary + CBS amplification CONFIRMED RHETORIC): 'long-term cooperation with China provides Iran with strategic diplomatic depth as it seeks an end to the war in the face of American pressure'; 'China is not merely an economic partner but part of Iran's political balancing against external threats'; 'China had paved the way for previous direct U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad'; 'predicted Beijing could emerge as a key player in ongoing diplomacy.' Publicly-sanctioned Iranian state-media-side scaffolding for China-mediation arc — institutional ground deliberately laid in Iranian state media for exactly the HEU-via-China structure Trump opened. Diplomatic architecture pivot from Russia-track-stalled to China-track-opening is now visible from BOTH sides. Wang Yi's May 6 meeting with Araghchi already had Beijing publicly calling for end to hostilities and Hormuz reopening — China's institutional position is consistent with custodian-broker role. Xinhua/Global Times SILENT on China-HEU-custodian door-opening Trump statement at filing — China letting meeting itself speak rather than commenting. Chatham House 'Trump-Xi: can progress be made on Iran?' May 12 framing carries forward as most articulated European analytical anchor. **A11 IRAN FM ARAGHCHI MET MODI AT BRICS-FM TABLE MAY 14 IN DELHI** (CONFIRMED — Iran Embassy + Indian press) — Araghchi sat at BRICS table with Chinese FM Wang Yi via BRICS framework on the day Trump in Beijing managing Iran with Xi face-to-face. China-Iran direct alignment via BRICS architecture, simultaneously, with US bilateral diplomacy in same building. **B1 OFAC ECONOMIC FURY EXTENDED — Treasury signalled prepared secondary sanctions on FIVE CHINESE TEAPOT REFINERIES (Hengli Petrochemical Dalian, Shandong Jincheng, Hebei Xinhai, Shouguang Luqing, Shandong Shengxing); CHINA MOFCOM MAY 2 ISSUED PROHIBITION ORDER UNDER BLOCKING RULES** (carries forward CONFIRMED) — sanctions architecture shifting from 'secondary risk' to 'explicit legal collision.' USD/CNY 6.78 (-0.1% 1D, PBOC stability into summit) supports China-demand proxies; Shanghai Composite 4,213.09 (-0.7% 1D, +5.7% 1M); Hang Seng 26,436.29 (+0.2% 1D). MSCI EM +2.1% today = clean market vote on Trump-Xi 'partners not rivals' opening. PRIOR (May 13): Al Jazeera + NPR led with 'doesn't need Xi'; PBS NewsHour + CSIS framed both leaders as 'intent on keeping the Iran war from overshadowing the summit' = itself a DOWNGRADE from prior Chatham House 'can progress be made on Iran' framing. 12+ US CEOs (Tim Cook, Elon Musk) travelling = TRADE-AND-TARIFFS CENTRE OF GRAVITY, not Iran.** Trump arrives evening May 13; summit May 14-15. **B1 OFAC ECONOMIC FURY EXTENDED — Treasury formally signalled prepared secondary sanctions on FIVE CHINESE TEAPOT REFINERIES (Hengli Petrochemical Dalian, Shandong Jincheng, Hebei Xinhai, Shouguang Luqing, Shandong Shengxing); CHINA MOFCOM MAY 2 ISSUED PROHIBITION ORDER UNDER BLOCKING RULES — FIRST FORMAL CHINESE LEGAL COUNTERMEASURE TO US SANCTIONS — barring recognition, enforcement or compliance inside China with US sanctions on those five refineries** (CONFIRMED — Treasury primary + MOFCOM order text + Holland & Knight + Baker McKenzie corroboration). Sanctions architecture shifting from 'secondary risk' to 'explicit legal collision' — raises cost for any Chinese intermediary doing parallel US-and-Iran business. For Indian importers and remaining Iranian-oil-shadow-trade pathways, raises bar on PRC re-export financing — a non-obvious cascade through Indian PSU oil refining. Trump arrives Beijing day after the Blocking Rules invocation — Chinese have public legal ground prepared. **A11 IRAN FM ARAGHCHI ARRIVES NEW DELHI MAY 13 FOR BRICS FM MEETING MAY 14-15 — ARAGHCHI WILL SIT AT BRICS TABLE WITH CHINESE FM WHILE TRUMP IS IN BEIJING signalling China-Iran direct alignment** (CONFIRMED — Iran Embassy + Indian press). Xinhua/Global Times SILENT on OFAC tranche and the China Blocking Rules — Chinese state press letting the legal action speak rather than commenting on it. USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1% 1D, PBOC stability into summit) supports China-demand proxies — Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6% same session = traders LONG China-reflation, SHORT war-premium-supply (Al -2.4%). Shanghai Composite 4,237.83 (+0.6% 1D, +6.8% 1M); Hang Seng 26,304.78 (-0.2% 1D). **The summit Iran agenda has been publicly downgraded: Trump's 'doesn't need Xi' line + CSIS framing 'intent on keeping Iran from overshadowing summit' = expectation set on Iran progress materially LOWER than last weekend.** Tasnim's Velayati Trump-Xi summit-timed jab from May 9-11 still operative ('never imagine you will enter Beijing triumphantly'). Iran framing Beijing summit as moment of Iranian leverage, not weakness. PRIOR FRAMING: Russia-China energy lifeline; Iran is ~13% of China imports; >50% of Chinese energy transits Hormuz. — Trump departs May 13 afternoon for Beijing summit with Xi; Iran on agenda; Trump will press Xi on China's financial support for Iran and 'dual-use goods.' Per Bloomberg sources, a major Iran military decision is unlikely before departure. China previously gave Sec Def Hegseth assurance (April 16) NOT to send weapons to Iran. The pause window (May 13 afternoon → May 15-16) coincides exactly with Trump 1-week clock close (May 14), effectively MEANING the clock-close CANNOT trigger an immediate kinetic response** (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg primary, SCMP, PBS, Al Jazeera, CNBC). **B7 NEW OFAC SANCTIONS WAVE MAY 11-12 PRIMARILY TARGETS IRAN→CHINA OIL CHAIN: 12 entities (4 Hong Kong, 4 UAE, 1 Oman) + 3 individuals over IRGC Shaid Purja'fari oil HQ + Golden Globe Turkey-cover company facilitating Iranian oil sales to China; builds on July 2025 Golden Globe designations** (CONFIRMED — Treasury primary). Treasury Sec Bessent: continue using sanctions to deprive Iran funding; May 12 designations added Iranian nuclear research with military applications. Real financial action (secondary-sanctions risk for downstream Chinese and Gulf intermediaries). USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1% 1D, -0.5% 1M, -1.7% 3M) — no current weaponization risk priced; if Trump-Xi summit fails on Iran/dual-use issue, USD/CNY could break 6.85+. Shanghai Composite 4,208.73 (-0.4% 1D, +5.3% 1M); Hang Seng 26,367.49 (-0.1% 1D). China May trade-data weakness contributes to Aluminum LME -2.3% counter-narrative (China property + global industrial moderation). **Xinhua/Global Times SILENT on OFAC sanctions tranche** (commercial reasons — China is primary buyer of Iranian oil with secondary-sanctions exposure). TASS/Pravda silent on WSJ UAE-Lavan story. Tasnim's Velayati (Iran Supreme Leader adviser) Trump-Xi summit-timed jab: 'Mr. Trump, never imagine that by taking advantage of Iran's calm, you will be able to enter Beijing triumphantly.' Iran framing Beijing summit as moment of Iranian leverage, not weakness. PRIOR FRAMING: Russia offered China energy lifeline (April 15) — pipeline + rail alternatives reduce but do not replace Hormuz exposure. Iran is ~13% of China imports; >50% of Chinese energy transits Hormuz.

active_triggers.condition: China-as-HEU-custodian framework formally accepted by Iran + US within 30 days (by 2026-06-13) — operational deliverable beyond joint statement rhetoric
active_triggers.note_2026-05-16: Branch pricing materially less favourable: Trump immediately reversed post-summit ('decimation' Truth Social + new May 16 letter); China silent post-summit (no MFA pushback). Joint statement WAS the deliverable, not a step toward one. Hold at `watching` until either side announces operational custodian framework.

active_triggers.condition: Iran-permitted Chinese transit through Hormuz exceeds 50 vessels/week sustained 2 weeks — turns selective-transit-as-diplomatic-instrument into routine operational pattern
active_triggers.note: 30+ Chinese ships transited since May 13 night (REPORTED Fars-IRGC primary). Threshold of 50/week sustained tests whether the May 13 reopening was Trump-Xi-summit-pause one-off or a structural Iran-China bilateral norm. If sustained, materially reframes the physical-blockade reading for non-Iranian non-Chinese commercial flow.

recent_signals.headline: **IRAN ALLOWED 30+ CHINESE SHIPS TO TRANSIT HORMUZ since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols'** (REPORTED — Fars/IRGC-Navy senior official primary; Iran state TV 'more than 30 ships'; Xinhua 'Iran begins letting some Chinese ships cross Strait of Hormuz' Chinese state amplification; Al Jazeera 'Trump, Xi discuss Strait of Hormuz as Chinese vessels transit key waterway'; CBS; Defense Post; Newsweek; Militarnyi; New Arab). Defense Post and Newsweek noted Iran has occasionally allowed Chinese ships even during the broader blockade because China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil. **The simultaneity with the Trump-Xi summit is the signal**: Xi's joint-statement line that he is 'interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait' co-exists with operational reliance on selective Iranian transit. **Trump immediately reversed post-summit ('decimation to be continued' Truth Social + Hannity 'not patient') and on May 16 sent Iran a new nuclear ultimatum letter** — the joint statement produced ZERO operational follow-through; China-as-HEU-custodian door is now operationally empty. **China silence post-summit (no MFA pushback on Trump's reversal)** — Beijing choosing not to engage Trump's contradictory rhetoric. The Aramco CEO Nasser benchmark ~30 vessels/day vs 140 pre-war is now partially explained: a portion is Iran-permitted Chinese transit, so non-Iranian non-Chinese transit is likely lower than headline figure. US markets sold off on the reversal: S&P -1.24%, NASDAQ -1.54%, Dow -1.07%, 10Y +14bp to 4.595%; tech sold off hard (Intel -6%, AMD -5.7%, Micron -6.6%, Nvidia -4.4%). Hang Seng -1.6% / Shanghai -1.0% — disappointment read absorbed but limited damage. USD/CNY 6.81 (+0.3% 1D); USD/CNY fix May 15 at 6.8415 vs Reuters 6.7976 = weaker-than-expected fix signaling tolerance for further yuan depreciation.
sources: Fars News (Iran semi-official) — IRGC-Navy senior official primary
Iran state TV — 'more than 30 ships'
Xinhua 'Iran begins letting some Chinese ships cross Strait of Hormuz' (Chinese state amplification)
Al Jazeera 'Trump, Xi discuss Strait of Hormuz as Chinese vessels transit'
CBS News + Defense Post + Newsweek + Militarnyi + New Arab
Times of Israel May 15 liveblog (Trump 'decimation' Truth Social)
Detroit News May 15 (Trump 'didn't ask China for any favours')
Trading Economics + CNBC (Hang Seng, Shanghai, CNY)
recent_signals.verification: 30+ Chinese ships transit = REPORTED ACTION (original Fars-IRGC primary, amplified through Chinese and Western state/wire chain; treat as one amplification cascade originally sourced to Iranian state attribution); Trump 'decimation' reversal = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (multi-source); China silence post-summit = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (no MFA pushback through May 16 morning); markets = CONFIRMED tape; USD/CNY fix = CONFIRMED (PBOC primary).

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP-XI SUMMIT DAY 2: JOINT STATEMENT ISSUED — IRAN 'CAN NEVER HAVE A NUCLEAR WEAPON' + STRAIT OF HORMUZ 'MUST REMAIN OPEN'** (CONFIRMED multi-cluster primary: Times of Israel May 15 liveblog, CBS News, CNN Politics, China MFA official readout fmprc.gov.cn 2026-05-14, Trump Fox News interview, Al Jazeera, Asian Mirror). Both sides committed to a 'constructive China-U.S. relationship of strategic stability' framework guiding the next three years. **Xi additionally opposed 'the militarization of the Strait' and any effort to 'charge a toll' for its use**, and said China is interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait — operationally meaningful given >50% of Chinese energy transits Hormuz today. **The China MFA readout records that 'China values Iran's pledge not to pursue nuclear weapons while affirming its legitimate right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy'** = Iran's right-to-civilian-program escape valve preserved in language. China-as-HEU-custodian door is now soft-confirmed at rhetorical-alignment level; neither side announced an operational custodian framework; Xi has NOT publicly accepted a custodian role. **Trump's Fox News follow-on assertions (CLAIMED — single source, NOT in joint statement or MFA readout)**: Xi 'offered to broker peace with Iran,' assured 'won't provide military equipment to Iran,' committed to buying US soybeans/oil/LNG + 200 Boeing 737s. Analyst skepticism: China's existing soybean appetite is limited by cheap Brazilian alternatives (Reuters via People News Today). **Xinhua and Global Times carried the joint statement on China's terms — Beijing letting the meeting + strategic-stability framework speak rather than commenting on Trump's Fox News follow-on claims.** USD/CNY fix May 15 at 6.8415 vs Reuters estimate 6.7976 = weaker-than-expected fix signaling Beijing tolerance for further yuan depreciation; Shanghai Composite 4,121.01 (-1.4% 1D); Hang Seng 25,897.05 (-1.9% 1D) — Hang Seng Trump-Xi disappointment read (Foreign Policy: 'failed to secure major concessions'). China April exports +14.1% y/y record $359.4bn, imports +25.3%; lending rates unchanged 11th straight month. The 'China-as-HEU-custodian framework formally accepted by Iran + US within 30 days (by 2026-06-13)' trigger remains 'watching' — joint statement is rhetorical alignment, NOT operational custodian framework.
sources: Times of Israel May 15 liveblog (joint statement primary)
CBS News liveblog
China MFA official readout (fmprc.gov.cn 2026-05-14) — official primary
CNN Politics liveblog
Al Jazeera Trump-Xi joint statement coverage
CNBC five-takeaways analysis
Trump Fox News interview (CLAIMED single source for Boeing/soybean/HEU follow-on)
Reuters via People News Today (analyst skepticism on soybeans)

recent_signals.verification: Joint statement text + China MFA readout = CONFIRMED multi-cluster; Iran 'can never have a nuclear weapon' + Hormuz 'must remain open' language = CONFIRMED in both readouts; strategic-stability framework = CONFIRMED; Trump Fox News follow-on (broker peace, no military equipment, soybeans, 200 Boeings) = CLAIMED only (single source, NOT in joint statement, MFA does not enumerate); MFA preservation of Iran 'peaceful nuclear energy' language = CONFIRMED (MFA readout primary); markets CONFIRMED tape.

recent_signals.headline: TRUMP-XI SUMMIT DAY 1 IN BEIJING — Xi opened with 'partners rather than rivals' / 'Thucydides Trap' framing + wants 2026 'landmark year' (CNBC + Al Jazeera + Times of Israel May 14 liveblog). **TRUMP PUBLICLY OPENED HEU-VIA-CHINA CUSTODIAN DOOR**: 'only Washington or Beijing can retrieve Iran's nuclear material if Tehran agrees to hand it over' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — ToI primary). Material policy signal — contradicts pre-departure 'doesn't need Xi' line; substitutes China for Russia as proposed HEU custodian on Day 15 of 21 Russia-track window. Xi has NOT publicly accepted any custodian role; framework remains stalled on substance. **B5 IRAN'S AMBASSADOR TO CHINA PUBLICLY FRAMED BEIJING AS 'POLITICAL BALANCING' PARTNER** (Tasnim primary + CBS amplification, CONFIRMED RHETORIC): 'China is not merely an economic partner but part of Iran's political balancing against external threats'; 'China had paved the way for previous direct U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad'; 'predicted Beijing could emerge as a key player in ongoing diplomacy.' Iran-state-media-side scaffolding for China-mediation arc — diplomatic architecture pivot from Russia-track-stalled to China-track-opening now visible from BOTH sides. **A11 ARAGHCHI MET MODI AT BRICS-FM TABLE IN DELHI MAY 14**, sitting alongside Chinese FM Wang Yi via BRICS framework — China-Iran direct alignment WHILE Trump in Beijing. Wang Yi's prior May 6 meeting with Araghchi already had Beijing publicly calling for end to hostilities and Hormuz reopening — institutional consistency with custodian-broker role. **Xinhua/Global Times SILENT on China-HEU-custodian door at filing — China letting meeting itself speak rather than commenting.** USD/CNY 6.78 (-0.1% 1D, PBOC stability into summit); Shanghai Composite 4,213.09 (-0.7% 1D, +5.7% 1M); Hang Seng 26,436.29 (+0.2% 1D); MSCI EM ETF +2.1% = clean market vote on Trump-Xi 'partners not rivals' opening. KOSPI +1.2% / Taiwan +0.9% participating in EM rally.
sources: Times of Israel May 14 liveblog (Trump HEU-via-China statement)
CNBC 'Xi asks Trump if U.S. and China can avoid Thucydides Trap'

ClickOnDetroit

CBS Live updates May 14

Al Jazeera 'Why BRICS FM meeting in India matters'

recent_signals.headline: TRUMP DEPARTED FOR BEIJING LATE MAY 12 DECLARING HE 'DOES NOT NEED XI'S HELP' ON IRAN — pre-summit posture hardened vs May 6-10 pulse-test; PBS+CSIS frame both leaders 'intent on keeping Iran war from overshadowing summit' = expectation DOWNGRADE; 12+ US CEOs (Cook, Musk) travelling = trade-and-tariffs centre of gravity. Summit May 14-15. **B1 OFAC ECONOMIC FURY EXTENDED — Treasury formally signalled prepared secondary sanctions on FIVE CHINESE TEAPOT REFINERIES (Hengli Petrochemical Dalian, Shandong Jincheng, Hebei Xinhai, Shouguang Luqing, Shandong Shengxing); CHINA MOFCOM MAY 2 ISSUED PROHIBITION ORDER UNDER BLOCKING RULES = FIRST FORMAL CHINESE LEGAL COUNTERMEASURE to US sanctions** (Holland & Knight + Baker McKenzie corroboration). Sanctions architecture shifting from 'secondary risk' to 'explicit legal collision.' **A11 IRAN FM ARAGHCHI ARRIVES NEW DELHI MAY 13 FOR BRICS FM MEETING MAY 14-15** — Araghchi sits at BRICS table with Chinese FM WHILE Trump is in Beijing = China-Iran direct alignment signal. Xinhua + Global Times SILENT on OFAC tranche + Blocking Rules — Chinese state press letting legal action speak rather than commenting. USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1% 1D, PBOC stability into summit); Shanghai Composite 4,237.83 (+0.6% 1D, +6.8% 1M); Hang Seng 26,304.78 (-0.2% 1D). Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6% same session = traders pricing 'trade-not-war' Trump-Xi summit reflation bid in industrial metals.

historical_summaries.summary: 4 signals. Chinese MFA spokesperson: attack and killing of Khamenei is 'grave violation of sovereignty | China provided missile spare parts, advanced radar systems, BeiDou-3 encrypted navigation for Iranian military, satellite intelligence suppo

10 signals. China is a NET BENEFICIARY of the crisis: (1) Manufacturing PMI 50 | China positioning as potential peace broker per CNN — Beijing moving from posturing to possible active mediation, which would be a major geo | China is net beneficiary of crisis (discounted Russian/Iranian crude)
